reviews.review_description:
Graphic is limited
Navigation is unclear
All in all in Looks Like a Test from a school team trying a Bit programming

This animal and nature playground app is a delight.   With Quest 3's improved passthrough it is very magical indeed for anyone who is a fan of exotic animals.  It's all very homespun but charming and the developer has clearly been working hard on making many different modes and there's a lot of variety and flexibility in this newest compilation.  The enlarging feature is fun, I like the table top room cause it seems to have all of the animals to choose from.  The UI isn't super pretty or intuitive right out the gate but once you learn how to navigate it it's all fairly easy to use and can also be played laying down. Thanks for the work you've put into this - it is a treasure for the 1 dollar price point!

2 notes to the dev - the trailer doesn't need to be so hectic, this is actually an extremely relaxing app.  And as someone else was pointing out the amount of similarly titled games is confusing.  This one seems to be the definitive as it has everything (and is also cheaper than some of the older, less-featured versions???).  You should streamline this somehow!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review!
Update: added a new UI level selection feature - it was a good idea!
Original response: A new update was just made with new features. Your feedback is good and for now we will try to keep pushing updates to all versions, although this is the extended version for sure.

reviews.review_description: When I got my Quest3 for my birthday, I played in the middle of the room and my family watched the casting on the TV. I wanted to show some passthrough stuff, so first I tried the built in alien shooting mixed reality demo. It was too long so become a little bored at the end. Then I tried The Thrill of the Fight, it was awesome of course. Then I got an idea and tried this game:) I got into the every animal on tables room and started to give small animals to everyone:) I put a shark to my girlfriend's hand and placed a cat and lizards in front of her. Also put cute animals next to my mom and put a tarantula on my brothers head:D And placed elephants and zebras on my father. Everyone enjoyed it very much. The animal models are very detailed compared to other Quest games. And the passthrough makes it super fun especially on the Quest3. I give it 4 stars because the game still needs some work, like saving your own levels, and making resizing animals more intuitive, but if updates come and it will feel less like an early access, I will change it to 5 stars. If you have a Quest3 and liked to play with plastic animals as a child and you are creative enough to entertain yourself without the game giving you goals, then definetly get this.

developer_response.body: An update was just made to be able to save object positions and for a custom mixed reality boundary for various areas, thanks for the feedback!

reviews.review_description:
This is a fun app and seems like a steal for the price paid. I have turtles and dolphins swimming round my room. then elephants and dinosaurs in my front room. lots of options from using a blank to spawn the animals you want, a decent amount of animals to pick from. or you can have some already preset and just watch them or move with controllers(didn't try hand tracking).
I can see it being relaxing.
This is simply the best. This is perfect to entertain family and friend with your Quest headset and the best Passthrough I have ever seen. Love everything about it. I give this a Supernova of stars.
